title: Context Pruning for Long Sessions
description:
Long agent sessions accumulate tool results that eventually overflow the context window. Port OpenClaw's context pruning pattern that automatically trims old tool results when context exceeds a threshold, preserving recent context while freeing up space.

## Source Pattern
- File: openclaw/src/agents/pi-hooks/context-pruning/pruner.ts
- Key: pruneContextMessages() with soft trim (head/tail chars) and hard clear modes
- Triggers when context exceeds configurable ratio of max window

## Implementation Plan
1. Create context_pruning.go in internal/agent/
2. Define pruning config: maxContextRatio (e.g. 0.8), softTrimChars, preserveRecentN
3. Implement EstimateTokenCount(messages) to estimate context size
4. Implement SoftTrimToolResult(result, headChars, tailChars) to truncate middle
5. Implement PruneContextMessages(messages, config) returning pruned messages
6. Integrate into agent turn pipeline to prune before sending to LLM
7. Add logging/metrics for pruning events
8. Add tests for pruning logic

## Acceptance Criteria
- Prunes oldest tool results first, preserving recent ones
- Soft trim keeps head/tail of large results, removes middle
- Hard clear removes entire tool results when soft trim insufficient
- Configurable thresholds via agent config
- Never prunes system messages or user messages
close_reason: Implemented two-phase context pruning: soft trim (head/tail) then micro-compact (hard clear). Integrated into agentic loop before each LLM call. Protects recent assistants and pre-user messages.

title: ACK Execution Fast Path
description:
When users respond with simple approvals like 'ok', 'do it', 'go ahead', 'yes', agents often waste a turn recapping what they're about to do instead of just doing it. Port OpenClaw's ACK execution fast path pattern that detects these approval patterns and injects an instruction to skip recaps and act immediately.

## Source Pattern
- File: openclaw/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts
- Key: ACK_EXECUTION_NORMALIZED_SET contains normalized approval patterns
- Function: resolveAckExecutionFastPathInstruction() injects 'Skip any recap or confirmation—just act immediately.'

## Implementation Plan
1. Create ack_fast_path.go in internal/agent/
2. Define normalized approval patterns (ok, do it, go, go ahead, yes, yep, yeah, sure, proceed, continue, approved, lgtm, ship it, etc.)
3. Implement IsAckExecutionPrompt(text string) bool to detect approvals
4. Implement GetAckFastPathInstruction() string returning the skip-recap instruction
5. Integrate into agent turn processing to prepend instruction when user message is an approval
6. Add tests for pattern detection

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detects common approval patterns case-insensitively
- Handles minor variations (punctuation, whitespace)
- Injects fast-path instruction into system context
- Does not false-positive on longer messages that happen to contain 'ok'
close_reason: Implemented ACK Execution Fast Path with normalizeAckPrompt, IsAckExecutionPrompt, GetAckFastPathInstruction. Integrated into buildAgentRunTurn. Supports 40+ approval patterns across 8 languages.

title: Embedding cache to reduce API costs
description:
Add an embedding cache that stores computed embeddings by content hash, avoiding redundant API calls to embedding services (OpenAI, Ollama, etc.).

**Schema (in SQLite backend):**
```sql
CREATE TABLE embedding_cache (
  provider TEXT NOT NULL,      -- e.g. 'openai', 'ollama'
  model TEXT NOT NULL,         -- e.g. 'text-embedding-3-small'
  provider_key TEXT NOT NULL,  -- API key hash for multi-tenant
  hash TEXT NOT NULL,          -- SHA256 of content
  embedding TEXT NOT NULL,     -- JSON array of floats
  dims INTEGER,                -- vector dimensions
  updated_at INTEGER,
  PRIMARY KEY (provider, model, provider_key, hash)
);
```

**Implementation:**
1. Add `internal/memory/embedding_cache.go`
2. `LoadEmbeddingCache(hashes []string) map[string][]float32` - batch load
3. `UpsertEmbeddingCache(entries []CacheEntry)` - batch upsert
4. `CollectCachedEmbeddings(chunks, cached) (embeddings, missing)` - helper
5. Integrate with Qdrant backend's embed() function
6. Content hash using SHA256 of normalized text

**Benefits:**
- Reduce embedding API costs by 50-90% for repeated content
- Faster indexing when re-processing sessions
- Works across sessions with same content

**Config:**
```yaml
memory:
  embedding_cache:
    enabled: true
    max_entries: 100000  # optional limit
```
close_reason: Implemented embedding cache in internal/memory/embedding_cache.go with full test coverage

title: SQLite memory backend with FTS5
description:
Implement a SQLite-based memory backend that replaces JSON file storage. This is the foundation for other memory features.

**Schema (matching openclaw):**
```sql
CREATE TABLE chunks (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  session_id TEXT,
  role TEXT,
  topic TEXT,
  text TEXT NOT NULL,
  keywords TEXT,  -- JSON array
  unix INTEGER NOT NULL,
  type TEXT,
  goal_id TEXT,
  task_id TEXT,
  run_id TEXT,
  episode_kind TEXT,
  confidence REAL DEFAULT 0.5,
  source TEXT,
  reviewed_at INTEGER,
  reviewed_by TEXT,
  expires_at INTEGER,
  mem_status TEXT DEFAULT 'active',
  superseded_by TEXT,
  invalidated_at INTEGER,
  invalidated_by TEXT,
  invalidate_reason TEXT,
  embedding TEXT,  -- JSON array for vector
  hash TEXT,       -- content hash for dedup
  model TEXT,      -- embedding model used
  updated_at INTEGER
);

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE chunks_fts USING fts5(
  id, text, topic, keywords,
  content='chunks',
  content_rowid='rowid',
  tokenize='unicode61'
);

-- Triggers to keep FTS in sync
CREATE TRIGGER chunks_ai AFTER INSERT ON chunks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO chunks_fts(rowid, id, text, topic, keywords) 
  VALUES (new.rowid, new.id, new.text, new.topic, new.keywords);
END;
```

**Implementation:**
1. Add `internal/memory/sqlite.go` implementing the Backend interface
2. Register as 'sqlite' backend in init()
3. Use `modernc.org/sqlite` for pure-Go SQLite (no CGO)
4. Add busy_timeout pragma for concurrent access
5. Implement all Backend methods using SQL queries
6. Use FTS5 MATCH for Search/SearchSession
7. Add migration from JSON index on first open

**Config:**
```yaml
memory:
  backend: sqlite  # or 'json-fts' for legacy
  path: ~/.metiq/memory.sqlite
```
close_reason: Implemented SQLite backend with FTS5 in internal/memory/sqlite.go with full test coverage

This replaces the current JSON file-based storage with a proper database backend that enables better performance, hybrid search, and reduced API costs through embedding caching.

title: Migrate auth-profiles.json (model API keys/OAuth)
description:
OpenClaw stores model provider credentials in ~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/agent/auth-profiles.json. This contains:
- API keys for Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.
- OAuth tokens for providers that use OAuth

Without migrating this, the agent cannot call LLM providers after migration.

Implementation:
1. Read auth-profiles.json from source agent directory
2. Merge API keys into config.json's 'providers' section
3. Handle OAuth tokens (may need special handling for refresh tokens)
4. Warn about any credentials that couldn't be automatically migrated

Security consideration: These are sensitive credentials - ensure proper file permissions (0600) on migrated files.
close_reason: Implemented in commit a9b603d

title: Import OpenClaw SQLite memory into swarmstr SQLite
description:
OpenClaw stores agent memory in SQLite (~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/memory/<id>.sqlite). Swarmstr now has native SQLite backend support (internal/memory/sqlite.go).

UPDATED APPROACH: SQLite-to-SQLite migration (not to JSON-FTS)

OpenClaw schema:
- chunks(id, path, source, start_line, end_line, hash, model, text, embedding, updated_at)

Metiq schema: 
- chunks(id, session_id, role, topic, text, keywords, unix, type, goal_id, task_id, run_id, episode_kind, confidence, source, reviewed_at, reviewed_by, expires_at, mem_status, superseded_by, invalidated_at, invalidated_by, invalidate_reason, embedding, hash, model, updated_at)

Implementation:
1. Add MigrateFromOpenClaw(srcPath string) method to SQLiteBackend
2. Map OpenClaw fields to Metiq fields:
   - id → id (MemoryID)
   - text → text
   - path → topic (use filename as topic)
   - source → source
   - embedding → embedding (if compatible dims)
   - updated_at → unix
3. Generate missing fields with defaults (session_id='migrated', confidence=0.5, mem_status='active')
4. Rebuild FTS index after migration
5. Add --migrate-memory-db flag to CLI

Reference: See SQLiteBackend.MigrateFromJSONIndex() for migration pattern.
close_reason: Implemented in commit a9b603d

title: Align admin /call auth config with startup file config
description: The LM Studio live daemon harness can now run against a deterministic in-process relay, but it still has to PUT /config before POST /call succeeds. Root cause: admin /call auth reads control policy from docsRepo.GetConfig(), while startup/file-loaded config initially only updates runtime configState. On a fresh local relay this leaves /call using secure defaults until config is persisted separately. Fix the daemon so startup/file-loaded config is the same source used by /call auth/readiness, then remove the harness-side /config bootstrap workaround.
close_reason: Made startup file config the persisted source of truth for admin /call, preserved secure control defaults on first boot when the file omits control, removed the live harness /config bootstrap workaround, and validated the LM Studio daemon harness end to end against local startup config.

title: Audit and harden runtime config loading/validation
description: Review swarmstr runtime config loading against the config spec/OpenClaw compatibility, ensure all declared fields are parsed and consumed correctly, and eliminate silent config failures by surfacing parse/validation errors prominently. Initial audit has already found missing parser support for several ConfigDoc fields (control, hooks, timeouts, session prune fields, provider api_keys, nostr channel nested config, etc.).

Filed follow-up swarmstr-h38r for remaining spec/docs drift around legacy session keys.

title: Fix custom provider prefix resolution for local models
description: autoResolveProviderOverride only recognizes hardcoded provider families (anthropic, openai, etc.) and returns empty for custom provider prefixes like lemmy-local/. This means models like lemmy-local/model.gguf never get matched to the providers config entry, even when providers.lemmy-local has a base_url. Additionally resolveModelProviderOverride never falls through to check agCfg.Provider when the model contains a slash.
close_reason: Fixed two issues in provider resolution: (1) autoResolveProviderOverride now extracts custom provider prefix from model strings (e.g. lemmy-local/ from lemmy-local/model.gguf) and looks it up in the providers map before returning empty. (2) resolveModelProviderOverride now has a final fallback that checks agCfg.Provider when auto-resolution fails, so explicit provider config is never ignored.

title: Compress tool results in conversation history
description: Beyond the existing tool_result_guard.go truncation (which caps individual results at call time), add post-hoc compression of tool results already in conversation history. During agentic loop iterations, older tool results that have already been processed by the model should be summarized down to their essential output (e.g. 'Read 200 lines from foo.go' instead of the full file content, 'Search found 5 matches in bar/' instead of all results). This is different from micro-compaction (which removes entire tool result messages after a time gap) — this preserves the message but compresses its content. Implementation: add a compressHistoricalToolResults() pass in RunAgenticLoop that walks messages and summarizes tool results older than N iterations. Reference: frontier pattern of 'return tool results as compact structured data, not verbose prose'.
close_reason: Added CompressHistoricalToolResults pass in the agentic loop that replaces older tool results with compact one-line summaries (preserving tool name, line/char counts, and content preview). Runs before micro-compaction on every iteration. Tool-specific summarizers for read, search, and command tools with generic fallback. 16 tests covering compression, edge cases, and summarization.

title: Enforce per-section prompt token budgets
description: Add hard per-section token budget allocation to prompt assembly. Currently swarmstr has ContextWindowTokens and tool result guards, but no formal budget partitioning. Each section (base system prompt, tools, retrieved memory, recent turns, dynamic context) gets a percentage of the total context window. Sections that exceed their budget get truncated/compacted independently. This prevents any single section (e.g. memory recall, tool definitions) from crowding out the rest. Implementation: add a PromptBudget struct to buildTurnPromptEnvelope that allocates percentages, measure each section against its budget, and truncate/warn when exceeded. Reference: frontier context engineering pattern of 'no section gets to eat the whole picnic basket'.
close_reason: Implemented per-section prompt token budget enforcement: added MemoryRecallMax (8%) and DynamicContextMax (12%) zones to ContextBudget, enforcement functions (EnforceSystemPromptBudget, EnforceDynamicContextBudget, EnforceMemoryRecallBudget), wired enforcement into turn_prompt_builder.go and memory_prompt.go, made session memory rune limits budget-proportional via SessionMemoryBudgetRunes(). 13 new tests added.

title: Gemini explicit context caching
description: Implement Gemini's CachedContent API for persistent prompt caching. Create a CachedContent resource containing the system instruction on first turn, then reference it via cachedContent field in subsequent requests. TTL configurable (default 1h). This is the biggest cost savings for Gemini since uncached input is billed at full price. Requires creating cached content via POST /v1beta/cachedContents and referencing it by name.
close_reason: Implemented Gemini CachedContent API integration. System instructions (and tools) are cached server-side via POST /v1beta/cachedContents with configurable TTL (default 1h). Local in-memory cache with SHA256 key tracks active resources and handles expiry. Cache is skipped for system prompts < 2000 chars. On cache hit, systemInstruction and tools are omitted from generateContent requests. 6 tests added.

title: Track cached input tokens in ProviderUsage
description: Add CachedInputTokens field to ProviderUsage and parse it from all three major providers: Anthropic (usage.cache_read_input_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens), OpenAI (usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens), and Gemini (usageMetadata.cachedContentTokenCount). This is the observability foundation for measuring whether prompt section caching and prefix stability actually improve cache hit rates. Log cache hit ratios on each turn.
close_reason: Implemented cached token tracking across all three providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini). Added CacheReadTokens and CacheCreationTokens to ProviderUsage and TurnUsage. Updated SessionStore.AddTokens to persist cache metrics. Added cache hit ratio logging. 4 tests added.

title: System prompt section caching
description: Cache computed system prompt sections between turns to improve provider prompt cache hit rates. Currently swarmstr rebuilds the entire system prompt every turn (bootstrap files, skills prompt, runtime context, memory system prompt) even though most sections are identical. Port the section caching pattern from src/constants/systemPromptSections.ts: cache stable sections, mark volatile sections for recomputation, clear caches on /compact and /clear. This directly improves prompt cache hit rates which reduces cost on every turn.
close_reason: Implemented prompt section caching with generation-based invalidation. Stable sections (bootstrap, agent prompt, runtime info, skills, docs, etc.) cached per-agent; tool summary computed fresh per-turn. Cache invalidated on config change, /compact, /clear, and session rotation.

title: Session memory compaction (LLM-free compaction)
description: Implement running session memory extraction that maintains a summary throughout the session. On compaction, use the pre-extracted session memory instead of making an expensive LLM summarization call. Keep last N messages (min 10K tokens, min 5 text-block messages, max 40K tokens). Preserve tool_use/tool_result pairs across the cut point. This eliminates the LLM-summarize cost entirely, making compaction instant. Critical for small context windows where every compaction call consumes precious budget. Port pattern from src/services/compact/sessionMemoryCompact.ts.
close_reason: Implemented LLM-free session memory compaction in internal/context/session_memory_compact.go with full test coverage. Integrated into main.go auto-compact and /compact handler.

title: Deferred tool loading (ToolSearch pattern)
description: Port the deferred tool loading pattern from src/. Tools marked as deferrable (MCP tools, low-frequency skills) should NOT be sent inline in every request. Instead, register them with a lightweight one-line description and let the model discover them via a ToolSearch-like tool when needed. Auto-threshold: when deferred tool definitions exceed 10% of context window tokens, activation triggers. This is the single biggest context reduction opportunity — tool schemas can burn 20-50% of the tool definition budget on tools the model never uses in a given turn.
close_reason: Implemented deferred tool loading: DeferredToolSet, PartitionTools, ToolSearch built-in tool, executor wrapper, agentic loop integration, and main.go turn builder integration. All tests passing.

title: Defer AlreadyProcessed marking until successful response
description: When the agent LLM fails or errors out during a DM turn, MarkProcessed is still called on the error path, which means the message is permanently skipped on recovery. The AlreadyProcessed store should only record an event after a successful response, so that transient LLM failures allow automatic retry within the since/replay window.
close_reason: Removed MarkProcessed from agent turn error path in runInboundTurn. Failed turns now leave the event unprocessed so the agent will retry the message within the replay window on recovery. Success path unchanged — MarkProcessed is still called only after a successful response and reply delivery.

title: Eliminate ~50 package-level global variables in daemon
description: cmd/metiqd/main.go declares ~50 package-level control* variables (controlAgentRuntime, controlDMBus, controlSessionStore, controlToolRegistry, controlSecrets, etc.) that form the daemon's shared state. These globals make the daemon untestable in isolation, create hidden coupling between subsystems, and make race conditions harder to detect. The globals should be replaced with a DaemonContext or similar struct passed through the call chain. Some already have mutex guards (controlDMBusMu, controlWsEmitterMu, controlPairingConfigMu) but many are accessed without synchronization.

All control_rpc_*.go, main_*.go, heartbeat_runner.go, acp_routing.go, dm_reply.go, agent_run_orchestrator.go, capability_sync.go, memory_scope.go, turn_prompt_builder.go, acp_inheritance.go, acp_cleanup.go, nip51_allowlist.go converted. daemonServices struct now has 5 sub-sections: relay, session, handlers, runtimeConfig, docsRepo. Remaining: Phase 3c (delete global vars from main.go var block once main.go internal references are also migrated).

Converted handleControlRPCRequest and all operational main.go code to use controlServices. Deleted 17 globals outright (converted to local vars). Remaining 35 globals kept for test compatibility (save/restore pattern) but have zero operational reads — all runtime access goes through controlServices.

title: Rename misleading controlPrivateKey variable to controlPubKeyHex
description: In cmd/metiqd/main.go, the package-level variable controlPrivateKey actually stores a PUBLIC key hex string (line 390: controlPrivateKey = pk.Hex() // always store pubkey only). Despite the misleading name, the code comment correctly notes it stores only the pubkey. This is a ticking time bomb for anyone reading this code — a developer might assume it contains a private key and handle it with inappropriate security caution, or worse, accidentally expose it thinking it's already public. The variable should be renamed to controlPubKeyHex and all 4 usage sites updated.
close_reason: Renamed controlPrivateKey to controlPubKeyHex across all 4 usage sites in cmd/metiqd/main.go. Build passes, tests pass (existing flaky test tracked separately).

This makes the code nearly impossible to test in isolation, reason about concurrency safety, or onboard new contributors. The file needs to be decomposed into focused packages (e.g. rpc_dispatch.go, session_lifecycle.go, config_mutation.go, plugin_ops.go, node_pairing.go, etc.) with proper dependency injection instead of global state.

title: Implement FIPSTransport satisfying DMTransport interface
description:
Create internal/nostr/runtime/fips_transport.go implementing the DMTransport interface over a FIPS mesh connection. This is the core integration point.

**Architecture**: The FIPSTransport wraps communication with a local fips daemon via its IPv6 adapter or (later) native API. Agent-to-agent messages are sent as FIPS datagrams addressed by the recipient's npub.

**Interface to satisfy** (from dm_transport.go):
```go
type DMTransport interface {
    SendDM(ctx context.Context, toPubKey string, text string) error
    PublicKey() string
    Relays() []string
    SetRelays(relays []string) error
    Close()
}
```

**Implementation approach — Sidecar mode (phase 1)**:
- SendDM: Connect to the destination agent's FIPS IPv6 address (derived from their npub → fd00::/8) over a simple TCP or UDP socket through the fips0 TUN interface. Use a lightweight framing protocol (length-prefix + JSON payload) to deliver the DM text.
- PublicKey: Return the agent's own hex pubkey (same key used by the FIPS node)
- Relays: Return empty — FIPS is relay-independent
- SetRelays: No-op for FIPS transport
- Close: Close any open FIPS connections

**Key design decisions**:
- Listen on a well-known FIPS port (e.g. FSP port 1337) for incoming agent messages
- Message format: reuse the existing DM JSON envelope so ACP/control bus code doesn't change
- Connection pooling: maintain a map[npub]*conn for reuse
- Health checks: use FIPS control socket (fipsctl show sessions) to verify peer reachability before sending
- Build-tag gated: //go:build experimental_fips

**Files**:
- internal/nostr/runtime/fips_transport.go (main impl)
- internal/nostr/runtime/fips_transport_test.go
- internal/nostr/runtime/fips_listener.go (inbound message listener)

**Compile-time check**:
```go
var _ DMTransport = (*FIPSTransport)(nil)
```
close_reason: FIPSTransport implemented: 7 files (3 source + 3 stubs + 1 test), 1066 lines, all 13 tests passing, both build modes verified

title: Add FIPS bootstrap config and feature gate
description:
Extend BootstrapConfig in internal/config/types.go and the config parser in internal/config/file.go to support FIPS configuration:

```go
// New fields in BootstrapConfig or a new FIPSConfig struct:
type FIPSConfig struct {
    Enabled       bool     `json:"enabled"`             // master switch
    Daemon        string   `json:"daemon,omitempty"`     // path to fips daemon socket or 'embedded'
    ControlSocket string   `json:"control_socket,omitempty"` // fipsctl control socket path
    Peers         []string `json:"peers,omitempty"`      // static FIPS peer npubs to connect to
    TransportPref string   `json:"transport_pref,omitempty"` // 'fips-first', 'relay-first', 'fips-only'
    BindAddr      string   `json:"bind_addr,omitempty"`  // UDP bind address for FIPS (default: 0.0.0.0:2121)
    EnableTUN     bool     `json:"enable_tun,omitempty"` // whether to use IPv6 TUN adapter
    UseNativeAPI  bool     `json:"use_native_api,omitempty"` // use native FIPS datagram API when available
}
```

Changes needed:
1. Add FIPSConfig to BootstrapConfig and state.ConfigDoc
2. Parse 'fips' section in file.go ParseConfigBytes
3. Add config validation in validate.go — FIPS requires a persistent identity (nsec must be set)
4. Add 'experimental_fips' Go build tag that gates compilation of FIPS-dependent code
5. Redact FIPS config appropriately in redact.go (no secrets to redact, but log the enabled state)
6. Add tests for parsing FIPS config section
close_reason: FIPSConfig type added to state.ConfigDoc with 8 fields (enabled, control_socket, agent_port, control_port, transport_pref, peers, conn_timeout, reach_cache_ttl). Config parser, validation, and bootstrap config extended. 11 new tests all passing. Full project builds cleanly.

title: Design FIPS integration architecture document
description:
Write an architecture doc in docs/ covering how FIPS integrates with swarmstr's existing networking layers. Must address:

1. **Transport layer mapping** — how FIPS FSP sessions map to swarmstr's DMTransport interface. The key insight: both systems use secp256k1 keypairs, so FIPS node identity IS the agent's Nostr identity. No identity bridging needed.

2. **Deployment models** — three options to document:
   - Sidecar mode: fips daemon runs alongside metiqd, agent uses IPv6 adapter (fd00::/8 TUN) transparently
   - Embedded library: link FIPS as a Go library via CGo FFI to the Rust core (heavier but tighter integration)
   - Native API mode: use FIPS's planned native datagram API (npub:port addressing) for zero-overhead agent messaging

3. **Transport selection strategy** — when to use FIPS vs relay-based transport. Propose a priority chain: FIPS direct mesh (if peer reachable) → relay-based NIP-17/NIP-04 fallback. Include health-aware routing.

4. **Feature gate design** — all FIPS support behind 'experimental_fips' config flag and build tag.

5. **Security model** — document the dual encryption benefit: FIPS hop-by-hop (Noise IK) + end-to-end (Noise XK), layered on top of ACP's existing NIP-44 encryption. Three layers of encryption for fleet messages.

6. **Wire compatibility** — ACP messages, control RPC, and fleet DMs are JSON payloads that don't care about transport. The same acp.Message struct works over FIPS datagrams as over relay DMs.

Output: docs/experiments/fips-integration-architecture.md
close_reason: Architecture document created at docs/experiments/fips-integration-architecture.md. Covers all 6 required sections plus framing protocol, fleet integration, ops considerations, and implementation phases. Oracle review applied: fixed security binding issue, corrected encryption layer count for FIPS paths, acknowledged control bus refactoring scope, added key derivation verification notes, documented key duplication risk, fixed framing length convention, and renamed FIPSNodeAddr to FIPSIPv6Addr.

- Update parity test fixtures

New types: AgentInternalEvent, InputProvenance, ExecApprovalSystemRunPlan, ExecApprovalMutableFileOperand. 38 camelCase aliases for wire compat. 14 new parity tests.

title: [Parity] OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions HTTP API
description:
OpenClaw's gateway now serves an OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions HTTP endpoint (openai-http.ts) supporting both streaming SSE and non-streaming modes. This allows external tools, IDEs, and services to interact with openclaw agents using the standard OpenAI API format. Swarmstr has no equivalent HTTP API surface.

OpenClaw features:
- POST /v1/chat/completions endpoint
- Streaming SSE with delta content chunks
- System/user/assistant message role handling
- Image content support (URL and base64)
- Configurable limits (body size, max image parts, total image bytes)
- Agent-scoped routing (model ID maps to agent)
- Session key generation per conversation
- Rate limiting and auth integration

Impact: This is a major interoperability surface. Many tools expect OpenAI-compatible APIs. Without this, swarmstr agents can't be accessed by the growing ecosystem of OpenAI API consumers.

Needs:
- HTTP handler in internal/admin or new internal/openaicompat package
- Chat completions request/response types
- SSE streaming support
- Message role mapping to agent command
- Image content extraction
- Rate limiting integration
close_reason: Implemented POST /v1/chat/completions endpoint in internal/admin/openai_compat.go with non-streaming JSON and buffered SSE streaming modes. Mounted on admin mux with auth. 19 tests passing.

title: [Parity] Nostr profile management (NIP-01 kind:0)
description:
OpenClaw's nostr extension now has full NIP-01 profile management: profile publishing to relays, profile import from relay network, profile merge, HTTP API for CRUD operations with rate limiting and URL safety validation, and state persistence. Swarmstr's nostr runtime has no profile management. This is needed for feature parity and interoperability with openclaw agents that publish/read profiles.

Key files in openclaw:
- extensions/nostr/src/nostr-profile.ts (create/publish/validate/sanitize profiles)
- extensions/nostr/src/nostr-profile-http.ts (HTTP handler with rate limiting)
- extensions/nostr/src/nostr-profile-import.ts (import from relays, merge)
- extensions/nostr/src/config-schema.ts (NostrProfileSchema with NIP-01 fields)

Needs in swarmstr:
- Profile struct and validation (name, displayName, about, picture, banner, website, nip05, lud16)
- Profile event creation (kind:0) with monotonic timestamps
- Publish to relays with result tracking
- Import from relays with timeout and relay selection
- Profile merge logic (local + imported)
- Admin API endpoints for profile CRUD
- State persistence for publish history
close_reason: Swarmstr already has superior profile management: nostr_profile (fetch kind:0 with caching), nostr_profile_set (fetch-merge-sign-publish), and nostr_resolve_nip05. OpenClaw only has HTTP API management, not agent tools.

title: Root-level container support (Docker/Podman/Quadlet)

title: Continuous proportional ComputeContextBudget
description: Replace the hard switch on ContextTier in ComputeContextBudget() with proportional math using lerp/clamp. All zones scale as fractions of effectiveChars with absolute floors and ceilings. Add MicroCompactKeepRecent and CompactionThreshold fields to ContextBudget. Add lerp/clampFloat64 helpers. Key fractions: bootstrap 32%, skills 7%, tools 13%, memory 5%, result share lerp(0.15,0.30), compaction threshold lerp(0.70,0.80).
close_reason: Replaced tier-switch ComputeContextBudget with continuous lerp/clamp math. Added CompactionThreshold, MicroCompactKeepRecent, CompressionPressure. All zones scale proportionally.

title: Add AgentConfig.ContextWindow field
description: Add ContextWindow int field to AgentConfig in models_config.go. Distinct from MaxContextTokens: ContextWindow is the model's actual window capacity, MaxContextTokens is an optional ceiling on assembled input. json:context_window,omitempty.
close_reason: Added ContextWindow field to AgentConfig, contextWindowForAgent helper, modelForAgent helper, updated turn_prompt_builder to use effective window

title: Universal micro-compaction in agentic loop
description: Remove the 'if cfg.ContextWindowTokens > 0' gate in RunAgenticLoop. Default to 200K when ContextWindowTokens is zero. Use budget.CompactionThreshold (proportional 0.70-0.80) and budget.MicroCompactKeepRecent instead of tier-gated values. All models now benefit from clearing stale tool results.
close_reason: Removed ContextWindowTokens > 0 gate — micro-compaction now runs universally for all models. Uses budget.CompactionThreshold and budget.MicroCompactKeepRecent for proportional scaling.

title: Epic: Universal Context Efficiency + Continuous Budget Scaling
description: Replace the rigid 3-tier (Micro/Small/Standard) context budget system with continuous proportional math. Apply token efficiency optimizations (micro-compaction, tool compression, skill rendering) to ALL model sizes. Add AgentConfig.ContextWindow for explicit deployment overrides. A 32K model should get different budgets than a 128K model — currently both get identical Standard treatment.
close_reason: Epic complete: all 10 implementation tasks done. Continuous proportional budget scaling replaces rigid 3-tier system. Universal micro-compaction, gradient tool compression, budget-driven compact prompts, SmallWindowEngine proportional budget.

Add inline micro-compaction before each Provider.Chat() call when estimated message chars exceed 75% of effective window. Pass model string through generateWithAgenticLoop to AgenticLoopConfig.ModelID.
close_reason: Implemented and compiling

title: Micro-compaction without LLM call
description: Create internal/agent/micro_compact.go. Port concept from src/services/compact/microCompact.ts. MicroCompactMessages() replaces old tool result content for compactable tools (web_fetch, web_search, memory_search, sessions_list, cron_list) with '[tool result cleared to free context]' marker. Oldest-first, preserving last KeepRecent results (Micro=1, Small=2, Standard=4). Does not mutate input slice. Stops when total chars drops below TargetChars. Tool name found by correlating ToolCallID backward to assistant message.
close_reason: Implemented and compiling

title: Auto-populate Turn.ContextWindowTokens from registry
description: Modify internal/agent/runtime.go to add modelID field to ProviderRuntime. In ProcessTurn(), auto-resolve Turn.ContextWindowTokens via ResolveModelContext(modelID) when the field is zero. Populate modelID from NewProviderForModel() or BuildRuntimeWithOverride(). Ensures all downstream budget-aware code gets correct window size automatically.
close_reason: Implemented and compiling

title: Context Budget Allocator (context_budget.go)
description: Create internal/agent/context_budget.go with ContextBudget struct and ComputeContextBudget() function. Allocates token budget across zones: bootstrap (25% capped at 60K), skills (5% capped at 30K), tool defs (10% capped at 12K), session memory, history (remaining), and tool result share (0.15 micro, 0.20 small, 0.30 standard). Micro 4K gets: bootstrap 4K chars, skills 800ch, tools 1.5K ch, history 4K ch. Small 8K gets: bootstrap 15K, skills 3.5K, tools 4K, history 10K. Includes 20% safety margin for token estimation drift.
close_reason: Implemented and compiling

title: Model Context Registry (context_window.go)
description: Create internal/agent/context_window.go with ContextTier (Micro/Small/Standard), ModelContextProfile struct, RegisterModelContextPattern() and ResolveModelContext() functions. Include built-in registrations for phi-3-mini (4K), gemma-2b (8K), llama-3.2-1b (4K), mistral-7b (8K), qwen2.5-0.5b (4K). Case-insensitive prefix matching with fallback to TierStandard (200K) for unknown models. This is the foundation - all other context management depends on it.
close_reason: Implemented and compiling

title: Epic: Sub-16K Context Management System
description: Enable swarmstr agent to operate on models with <16K context windows (Phi-3-mini 4K, Gemma-2B 8K, Llama-3.2-1B, etc.) with minimal service degradation. Currently the system assumes 32K-128K+ windows with DefaultBootstrapTotalMaxChars=150K, skill budgets of 30K chars, and 30 agentic loop iterations. This epic introduces a budget-zone allocation system driven by a model-context registry, scales all content-injection paths proportionally, adds micro-compaction, and introduces a SmallWindowEngine. All changes are additive - existing large-model behavior is fully preserved.
close_reason: All 13 implementation tasks completed. Full sub-16K context management system implemented and tested.
